sprintNotes: GOAL: Harden the pipeline so the next post-Sprint-14 drill captures uninterrupted ~10 min from cold start, tagged correctly, with DTC/runtime/fuel-status context, and pushes cleanly Pi -> server without manual bypass. Session 23 milestone proved the pipeline works end-to-end but surfaced 4 TDs + revealed gaps (23s actual data window, no data_source tagging, missing fuel-status/MIL/runtime PIDs, no drive_id grouping). Sprint 14 closes those gaps.

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprints 10-13). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi work. CIO does NOT need to be in the car for any of Sprint 14 -- bench-only sprint. The next drill is Sprint 15+.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: main at 85fca8b (Sprint 13 merged). Pi runs systemd eclipse-obd in --simulate mode (still). OBDLink LX paired/bonded/trusted (persistent from Session 23 -- DO NOT re-pair). /dev/rfcomm0 binding DID NOT survive the post-Session-23 reboot (US-167 carryforward owes reboot-survival). .env has OBD_BT_MAC=00:04:3E:85:0D:FB (actual MAC -- TD-023 will fail any fresh main.py launch until US-193 lands). data/obd.db on Pi has 149 real-Eclipse rows from Session 23 drill + Session 23 post-sync state -- DO NOT DELETE before US-197 regression-fixture export.

SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy at 10.27.27.10. 176 rows from Session 23 drill persisted (149 realtime_data + 11 statistics + 16 connection_log). Server-side schema migrations needed for US-195 (data_source) + US-200 (drive_id).

SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-harden (created by PM from main@85fca8b). Marcus committed this setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-19 (PM, CIO-directed): Sprint 14 = 12 stories (4S + 8M = 20 size-points; US-202 + US-203 added Session 24 to fold TD-027 timestamp fix at front of sprint per Ralph inbox notes 2026-04-19-from-ralph-td027-not-on-sprint14.md and 2026-04-19-from-ralph-us202-additional-writers.md, both approved by PM). BIGGER than any prior Pi sprint. Per CIO: 'add in based on what you know you can pick the priority. I would focus on high value items first.' Grouped into 4 priority bands: P0 (gates next drill) -> P1 (data quality + carryforward) -> P2 (display) -> P3 (hygiene).

EXECUTION ORDER (recommended; Ralph has latitude to rearrange within priority band): P0 FIRST -- US-202 (timestamp foundation, COMPLETE per Ralph US-202 close), then US-203 (timestamp sweep -- the 8 additional naive writers Ralph audited), then US-195 data_source column (depends on US-202 + US-203), then US-193 (TD-023 fix; gates fresh-Pi main.py), then US-194 (TD-025+26 fix; gates fresh-Pi sync_now.py). P2 -- US-198 TD-024 fix (status_display GL crash), then US-192 display-real-data retry (depends on US-198). P3 LAST -- US-201 B-044 audit + API_KEY deploy bake-in (mechanical sweep, no runtime risk).

FRAMING CORRECTION TO ABSORB (Spool framing vs CIO actual; partially explained by TD-027): Spool reported ~23s of OBD-connected data window across 2 connection windows; CIO states actual drill was several minutes of idle. Both can be true if connection_log only logs OPEN/CLOSE (the gap between events != wall-clock span). US-202 includes a Thread 1 investigation sub-task to determine whether the discrepancy is gap-between-events (most likely), format-mix-corrupts-delta, or other. Pipeline proven + tune dialed (LTFT=0%) regardless. Post-Sprint-14 drill needs uninterrupted ~10 min capture from COLD START to produce a tuning-review-grade datalog (separate from TD-027 fix; the next drill needs to be longer regardless).

stories.title: Fix TD-027: timestamp accuracy + format consistency in capture tables
stories.intent: Three coexisting timestamp formats in Pi capture tables (DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP space-separator, Python explicit with mixed tz semantics, sync_log.py ISO-8601 T...Z) corrupt time-window queries (BETWEEN on lexicographically-mixed strings returns wrong rows). Spool's '23s data window' framing dispute (CIO says actual was several minutes) is partially explained by gap-between-events on connection_log + possibly format-mix delta corruption. Filed at CIO direction Session 24 by Ralph. Two stories on Sprint 14 depend on trustworthy timestamps: US-195 (data_source filter on time window) + US-197 (regression fixture export uses BETWEEN). LAND FIRST -- gates US-195 + US-197.

invariants: Do NOT break the exemption list -- specs/docs/memory/tests-of-the-lint are the documented exemptions; narrow the scope aggressively.
Do NOT generate a new API_KEY if one already exists -- idempotent; overwriting breaks Pi/server pairing.
Do NOT require the audit script to be Pi-side -- Windows + Linux both need to run it (portable bash OR python).
Do NOT commit the API_KEY -- it lives in .env, never in git.
Do NOT scope-creep -- this is audit + sweep + API_KEY bake-in. Do NOT add OAuth, rotation policy, or key-management service (Sprint 15+ at earliest).
If the audit surfaces a large amount of leftover literals, STOP and file findings to PM -- do not silent-sweep >20 files without PM ack.
stopConditions: STOP if audit surfaces >20 literal-addresses in non-exempt files -- surface to PM first; may need scope decision (sweep all now vs file follow-up stories).
STOP if deploy-pi.sh API_KEY handling conflicts with existing COMPANION_API_KEY logic -- may be already partially implemented; audit first.
STOP if generate_api_key.sh clipboard helper requires xclip/pbcopy that isn't cross-platform -- drop clipboard feature, keep stdout only.
STOP if lint test cost exceeds 1s -- move to slow-suite or skip in CI pre-commit to keep fast-suite fast.
